description: Date and time when the theoretical signal was observed.
Observed market symbol.
Chart timeframe used for the observation.
Cost profile to test the observation under.
Manual market context classification.
Theoretical direction of the observed signal.
Theoretical entry price. This is not an executed entry.
Theoretical stop price. For SHORT it must be above entry. For LONG it must be below entry.
Theoretical target price. For SHORT it must be below entry. For LONG it must be above entry.
Manual price level that invalidates the idea.
Why the observation should be invalidated if that level is reached.
Person or process responsible for manual review.
Manual notes about context, setup quality, liquidity, Fibonacci, Elliott or invalidation.
Whether the observation is already resolved at intake time.
Observed theoretical exit price if the observation is already resolved.
Reason why the observation was resolved.
Maximum favorable movement in R before resolution.
Maximum adverse movement in R before resolution.
Number of bars from observation to resolution.
Manual source label. Example: TradingView manual observation.
Optional screenshot path for later review.
